calendar.matters:
SMALL CLAIM APPEALS
Off Calendar Oct-24-2006 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: JPT Michael Dickstein, Clerk Karissa Shaw, not reported. (525)

APPEARANCES:
Timothy J. Walton appearing for appellant Daniel L. Balsam (Appellant is also present).
Bennet G. Kelley appearing for respondent Valueclick, Inc.

This cause comes on regularly for trial de novo. Appellant is sworn.

The court hears argument from counsel on the issues of the case, mainly the jurisdiction of the case, preemption of California spam law, and whether proper notice was given to parties.

Counsel provides documentation to the court to support their arguments.

The following exhibits were marked and admitted into evidence;
Respondent's Exhibit #1 - FedEx Track Shipments printout, dated 9/20/06, 1 page.
Respondent's Exhibit #2 - Dan Balsam e-mail, dated 7/14/06, 2 pages.

Appellant's Exhibit #A - 2 page chart titled CAN-SPAM Act Preemption of State Laws.
Appellant's Exhibit #B - "Why Does Email Bounce?", print-out from BusinessKnowHow.com, 6 pages.

At the conclusion of the hearing, the matter is taken under submission.

10/24/06 JUDGMENT RENDERED.
